feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Journal entry type not editable in the Reprocess Bank Statement Items App
entry.content:
Hello,

in the App Reprocess Bank Statement Items we normally can edit the Journal entry type. For some items, it is not editable.

What can we do, that we can change the fixed journal entry type?

The field Journal entry type is greyed out.

Please help.

Kind regards

Fabian

entry.title: Request for Global/Regional Catalog for App: Aging Report - Accounts Receivable
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support Team,

We are currently trying to deploy the Aging Report - Accounts Receivable app for our Ireland territory.

Upon review, we found that this app is currently only available in country-specific catalogs for Japan and China. We need to enable access for users in the Ireland region, but the app is not appearing in the standard catalog for that territory.

Could you please advise if there is a global catalog we should be using, or provide guidance on the correct procedure to make this app accessible for the Ireland territory?

Thanks, Sravan

entry.title: Organizational Change report shows no data after simulation
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, an organizational change simulation completes successfully but no data is shown in the Organizational Change Report Financial Data app.

Users may expect that once a simulation runs successfully, results should appear in the report. However, even when simulation data or postings exist, the report can still show no data.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Is it possible to add a custom field to sap maintenance assistant?
entry.content:
Hello, we are trying to add a custom field to the SAP Maintenance Assistant that we created in Cloud ERP. We are at the start of our search and we are not even sure if the maintenance assistant is extendable.

Has anyone any experience with it?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: How to copy custom extension field value to standard Product field in SO
entry.content:
Environment: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

BUSINESS REQUIREMENT:
Industry requirement where ProductDescription (40 chars) is insufficient. A custom field (140 chars) stores the full product name on the Product master. A custom search field on Sales Document Item allows users to search by this extended name and select a product.

SCENARIO:
1. Custom field YY1_CUSTFIELD_SDI on Sales Document Item (Code List based on CDS View)
2. Custom VH searches products by extended name field
3. User selects product → YY1_CUSTFIELD_SDI populated with Product ID ✅
4. Need: automatically copy this to standard Product field ❌

WHAT WE TRIED:

1. MDE additionalBinding on C_SalesOrderItemManage→ "Use of CDS Entity not permitted" #CUSTOMER layer

2. BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_BUSINESS

→ Can write standard Product field ✅

→ Cannot read E_SALESDOCUMENTITEMBASIC ❌

3. Extension BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM
→ Can read SALESDOCUMENTITEM_EXTENSION_I ✅
→ Cannot write standard Product field ❌

4. KUE auto-generated MDE (/1BCWDY/ namespace)
→ Not editable ❌

5. D_SalesOrderItemProductCVH
→ @Metadata.allowExtensions missing ❌

QUESTION:
Is there a supported BAdI or extensibility approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to copy a value from a custom extension field to the standard Product field on Sales Order Item?

SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: PSM Budget Availability Control
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team

I’d like to flag a functional point that we believe should be formally raised with SAP, as it impacts the design of budget blocking and availability control for grant‑related scenarios.

During workshops with Controlling, a requirement from external funders was confirmed: budget blocking (availability control) is expected at cost category level. In the current design, cost categories are driven by GL accounts based on the funder.

However, the standard AVC capabilities in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, budget blocking by cost category (GL‑based) is not supported:

- AVC via WBS elements was considered but is not a viable operational solution.

- AVC via hierarchy was also discussed, but does not fully meet the funder requirement to control budget at cost category level.

This is therefore a question around standard SAP capabilities and design limitations.

Can you please :

- Validate if any standard SAP pattern exists to meet this requirement.

- Obtain confirmation in case this requirement cannot be covered by standard SAP.

Best Regards,

Djalil

SAP Manager

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud] Regarding the assembly and consumption of item type FERT
entry.content:
The BOM includes a product of item type FERT.

This FERT product is produced on a forecast basis, with a pre-registered Production Indicator (PIR).

We want the PIR to be consumed when a sales order for the parent product is received, but it's not being consumed.

It was consumed when the item type was HALB.

The settings are: Policy: 70, Dual MRP Classification: 1, Consumption Mode: 4, Forward/Reverse Consumption Period: 365.

How can I make the PIR of a component product like a semi-finished product be consumed when it's a FERT product?

entry.title: Standard Return Order - Issues
entry.content:
We have the below issues for Standard Return Order

  1. If we want to create a Standard Return Order of 100+ line items, it becomes difficult to put 4-6 field mandatory data for each and every line item as shown in the attached file.
  2. The auto creation of Delivery and Invoice should be stopped.
  3. Credit Memo should not be posted automatically.
  4. FOC should be enable for Lean Return Order.

Please provide us with a solution for SAP S/4 Hana public cloud.

CB User - CB9980000000

entry.title: Unable to assign zero value to dummy SKU for shipping-only refund (S/4HANA Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi team,

We are integrating Shopify with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and using the Credit Memo Request (A2X API) to process refunds. Our requirement is to refund only shipping charges, without refunding the product. Based on earlier guidance, we created a dummy material (SKU) to represent the refund item.

However, we are facing an issue where we are not able to assign a value of 0.00 to the dummy SKU (base price condition PPR0). The system requires a positive value (e.g., 0.01).

Our questions are:

  1. Why is a dummy SKU required instead of using the original material from the invoice?
  2. Is there a supported way to allow zero-value pricing for this dummy item?
  3. If a separate pricing procedure is required for refunds, how can it be assigned in Public Cloud given that new document types cannot be created?

Any guidance or best practices for this scenario would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: The condition class in S4 HANA is not created BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 but it is in R3
entry.content:
Good morning everyone.
Currently, my client is in the process of migrating to S/4HANA. We have a program Z that creates sales orders (VA01) through the BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2. The program fills in all the required fields of the BAPI (it enters four condition classes per item), but after creating the order, it does so without the ZCGM condition, which is manually configured as mandatory for a specific order class, ZC06. If I create the order manually using VA01, SAP prompts me to enter the ZCGM condition class since it is mandatory, and it creates the other classes without problems.

The curious thing here is that this same BAPI is used in R/3, and the same fields are filled in, but the order is created using the ZCGM condition class.

I have seen that at the configuration level, everything is OK in both R/3 and S/4HANA. I performed standard debugging on the BAPI and verified that the condition is never lost, but for some reason, it's not created in S4 HANA when other condition classes are added. If only the ZCGM class is used, it creates it without problems, but the client needs to add other condition classes as well.

S4 HANA order created through BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2  (The ZCGM condition is not present.)

Order created in R3 with the same data and using BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2( if created with condition ZCGM )

 

Data that I enter in conditions for the BAPI in both S4 HANA and R3 environments.

We have also tried setting a value in LOGICAL_SWITCH-PRICING but it doesn't work for us in S4 HANA either.

entry.title: Split Valuation with Automatic Batch (Category ‘X’) – Valuation Type Issue in STO (ZUB)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have configured Split Valuation with activation at the valuation area level. For our materials, we are using valuation category ‘X’ (Automatic Batch), where each batch is automatically treated as a separate valuation type. This configuration is working as expected in standard scenarios.

However, we are facing an issue during the creation of a Stock Transport Order (STO) with the following details:

  • Document Type: ZUB
  • Material: 1400000005
  •

entry.title: Sales Organization address fields in in Email Templates
entry.content:
Hello,

Due to business requirements, there's the need to use sales organization address fields (Name 1, Name 2, etc...), however I cannot find these fields when extending the standard email template SD_SLS_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE and creating my own custom email template.

Are these fields available? If not, is there any other way to use it?

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: BC Upgrade TR import issue
entry.content:
BC Upgrade TR and BC scope extention TR (Owner CBC) on public cloud error while importing on QA/Test and PRD both.
Unable to re-import or manual import. Please advise.

entry.title: Supplier invoice clear issue-items have not been activated due to inconsistent withholding tax info
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When we generated the invoice, the WHT details had not been entered in the 'Supplier Master.' However, we have now updated the WHT details in the Supplier Master. After entering the WHT information, we attempted to clear this invoice using a 'down payment' via the 'Clearing Outgoing Payments' application. At this stage, we encountered an issue; we received an error message stating, "Items were not activated due to inconsistent withholding tax information."

Based on my analysis, it appears that the absence of WHT details at the time the invoice was generated is the root cause of this problem.

How can we resolve this issue? Please provide appropriate advice.

Thanks & Regards,

DNR

entry.title: Provide a CDS view for Overhead rate in Costing Sheet
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

My project utilizes a costing sheet based on overhead quantity. At the end of each month, a step must be taken to input the actual overhead rate into the system, based on the overhead group maintained. This allows the customer to request a report to verify the actual overhead rate. Please provide a CDS view for the overhead rate in the costing sheet that you have released in the SAP public cloud.

Warm regards,

Chi

entry.title: Is there any api that can approve purchase orders in public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi All

We need to approve purchase oder through an extranal system,

Is there any api available in public cloud that supports purchase order

approval , or is there another way to handle approvals via en extranal system?

I found the nots 3551832 - Purchase Requisition approval via API and that mentions Purchase Requisition not have api for approval .However,it does not mention whether purchase oder are supported.

entry.title: Depreciation Start Date Requirement – Beginning of Fiscal Year (Irrespective of Capitalization Date)
entry.content:
We have a business requirement where the depreciation start date of an asset should always be set to the beginning of the fiscal year, irrespective of the capitalization month.

For example:

  • Capitalization Date: 15-Nov-2026
  • Expected Depreciation Start Date: 01-JAN-2026 (Start of Fiscal Year)

    We would like to confirm:

    1. Whether it is possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) to automatically set depreciation start date as start of fiscal year for all assets.
    2. If yes, please confirm the standard configuration steps.
    3. If not, is there any:

entry.title: Cost Center Derivation on Non-Cost Element GR/IR Account (SAP Cloud ERP)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, a GR/IR account that is not defined as a cost element (i.e., no primary/secondary cost element category maintained) is unexpectedly deriving a cost center during journal entry posting. 

Analysis Performed:

  • Verified that the GR/IR account is not maintained as a cost element in controlling.
  • Checked configuration under account assignment settings and document splitting – no relevant configuration found that would enforce cost center derivation.
  • Reviewed substitutions/validations (if applicable) – no rule identified causing this behavior.
  • No custom logic (BAdI / extensibility) impacting account assignment.

We kindly request confirmation on whether this behavior is standard in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. In case this is not the intended standard behavior, please provide a suitable solution approach, relevant SAP Note, or correction instructions. Additionally, we would appreciate guidance on any recommended configuration settings or control mechanisms to prevent similar occurrences in the future.

BR,
Evrim

entry.title: Workflow Trigger Behavior After Project Approval
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding the standard workflow behavior for project approval.

If the workflow is triggered when a user changes the project status to “Released,” what happens after the approval is completed?
If the user modifies the project data again afterward, will the workflow be triggered again?

Additionally, if we plan to extend the approval process to an external BPM system, what would be the recommended approach to prevent the project creator from manually releasing the project?

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Customizing issue - Person Responsible
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA FIORI Cloud Public we have an issue regarding the order transfert between both systems : A8L(100 and 120) to A7F(120).

Customizing for :

  • « Person Responsible », ID 102966

is done in A8L/100, copied in A8L/120 but not copied in A7F/120 because we do not have any no transport order.

At saving, information message is displayed « Configuration changes are not recorded to a transport »
